issues.title: Incorrect revenue calculation
issues.problem: The query calculates lifetime value without considering the transaction status.
issues.fix: SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as lifetime_value

issues.title: Missing filter for total orders
issues.problem: The query counts all transactions without considering the status.
issues.fix: COUNT(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.transaction_id ELSE NULL END) as total_orders

issues.title: Missing comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the logic and calculations.
issues.fix: Add comments to explain the purpose of each part of the query.

issues.title: Inconsistent naming
issues.problem: The naming of aliases and columns is inconsistent.
issues.fix: Use consistent naming conventions for aliases and columns.

review.corrected_sql:
SELECT c.customer_name, c.email, c.tier,
       SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as lifetime_value,
       COUNT(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.transaction_id ELSE NULL END) as total_orders
FROM dim_customer c
LEFT JOIN fact_transactions t ON c.customer_id = t.customer_id
WHERE t.status = 'COMPLETED'
GROUP BY c.customer_name, c.email
HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) > 1000
ORDER BY lifetime_value DESC;
review.summary: The query had issues with revenue calculation and total orders count. It also lacked comments and had inconsistent naming.
